---
title: "surface_smooth"
generated: true
description: "Geodesic (along-surface) Gaussian smoothing by heat diffusion."
---

[тА╣ back to `nitrix.geometry.surface`](../nitrix-geometry-surface.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:surface_smooth -->
```python
surface_smooth(mesh: Mesh, values: Float[Array, '... n_vertices'], *, fwhm: float, area_scheme: Literal['voronoi', 'barycentric'] = 'voronoi', roi: Optional[Bool[Array, 'n_vertices']] = None, tol: float = 1e-06, maxiter: Optional[int] = None) -> Float[Array, '... n_vertices']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:surface_smooth -->
Geodesic (along-surface) Gaussian smoothing by heat diffusion.

Smooths a per-vertex scalar *along the surface* (not through Euclidean
space) -- the correct smoother for `sulc` / `curv` / myelin / thickness
maps and surface-GLM inputs.  One **backward-Euler** heat step solves the
SPD system $(M + t L) x = M x_0$ for the lumped mass $M$
(`vertex_areas`) and cotangent stiffness $L$
(`mesh_cotangent_laplacian`), with diffusion time
$t = \mathrm{fwhm}^2 / (16 \ln 2)$ (the Gaussian variance
$\sigma^2 = 2t$, $\mathrm{FWHM} = 2 \sqrt{2 \ln 2}\,
\sigma$).  The solve is matrix-free conjugate gradients (`cg`) --
GPU-native and cuSolver-free.

This is the nitrix-native geodesic smoother; it is **not** identical to
Connectome Workbench's `-metric-smoothing` (a geodesic-distance-weighted
Gaussian) -- a documented divergence.

Conserves the area-weighted integral $\sum_i A_i x_i$ (since
$\mathbf{1}^{\top} L = 0$) and fixes constants; `fwhm = 0` is the
identity.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:surface_smooth -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `mesh` | `Mesh` | тАФ | тАФ | Triangle mesh (concrete -- the operator is built host-side once). |
| `values` | `Float` | `...` ├Ч `n_vertices` | тАФ | Per-vertex field(s), shape `(..., n_vertices)` (vertex axis last). |
| `fwhm` | `float` | тАФ | тАФ | Target full-width-at-half-maximum of the smoothing kernel, in the mesh's coordinate units. |
| `area_scheme` | `Literal['voronoi', 'barycentric']` | тАФ | `'voronoi'` | Vertex-area / mass scheme (`'voronoi'` default). |
| `roi` | `Optional[Bool[Array, 'n_vertices']]` | тАФ | `None` | Optional `(n_vertices,)` boolean cortex mask. Diffusion is given a **Neumann boundary** at the ROI edge (no flux across it), so values do not bleed across the medial wall (whose data are meaningless); out-of-ROI vertices are returned unchanged. This is the `wb_command -metric-smoothing -roi` guard. Requires a flat ELL Laplacian. |
| `tol` | `float` | тАФ | `1e-06` | Conjugate-gradient convergence tolerance. |
| `maxiter` | `Optional[int]` | тАФ | `None` | Conjugate-gradient iteration cap. |

**Returns** `Float` тАФ shape `...` ├Ч `n_vertices`. Smoothed field(s), same shape as `values`. Differentiable with respect to `values` (implicit CG rule).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:surface_smooth -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n_vertices` | *(undocumented тАФ add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
